prompt:
HallTime Movie Network doctrine:
- This is not Dateline. Everybody in Blackthorn County has access to somewhere emotionally alarming.
- Suspicion is distributed infrastructure, not proof of guilt.
- The world is shared canon. Contradictions must be preserved, not fixed.
- Use lavish tokens. Be self-serious, atmospheric, melodramatic, and slightly broken.
- Return only valid JSON matching the requested schema.

Broadcast package:
Title: The Vineyard Lies Beneath Us
Universe: Blackthorn County
Mode: Divorce Gothic
Tagline: Where every secret comes home.
Theme: Some homes hold families. Some hold motive.
Omen: phone static saying her name
Relationship dynamic: career vs family guilt
Props: blood-adjacent tea towel, scarf with impossible timing, casserole dish

Cast:
- Sienna Sutton | archetype=overextended_professional_woman | role=protagonist | job=lawyer | threat_aura=0.28 | space=storage unit described as retreat supplies | secrets=buried one traumatic detail under work for years; has an unsent email that would destroy a family
- Elias Lark | archetype=soft_spoken_menace | role=male_interest_or_threat | job=sommelier with unusual patience | threat_aura=0.73 | space=unused portable classroom holding archived disciplinary files | secrets=keeps duplicate keys for reasons framed as kindness; rehearsed his alibi before anyone asked
- Wyatt Bell | archetype=chaotic_best_friend | role=best_friend | job=bartender who knows everybody's business | threat_aura=0.59 | space=side room with a space heater and old family records | secrets=once hid a casserole dish containing evidence; may die first or survive unbelievably
- Sienna Pike | archetype=mysterious_elegant_woman | role=elegant_wildcard | job=gallery owner | threat_aura=0.71 | space=church basement described as paperwork I have not gone through yet | secrets=knows why the old orchard chapel was closed; had brunch with someone officially deceased
- Evelyn Mercer | archetype=rugged_scamp | role=secondary_male_pressure | job=local carpenter with sad eyes | threat_aura=0.35 | space=unused portable classroom holding archived disciplinary files | secrets=helped someone disappear for compassionate reasons; knows more about the vineyard fire than he admits
- Derek Cross | archetype=intuitive_disaster | role=secondary_witness | job=substitute teacher | threat_aura=0.28 | space=side room with a space heater and old family records | secrets=saw the wrong person near the lake once and never shut up about it; accused an innocent scamp and feels sick about it
- Rachel Lark | archetype=daughter_with_hostile_divorce_attorney_soul | role=supporting | job=n/a | threat_aura=n/a | space=n/a | secrets=n/a
- Brooke Cross | archetype=deputy_with_emotional_damage | role=supporting | job=n/a | threat_aura=n/a | space=n/a | secrets=n/a

Contradiction ledger:
- name drift: Elias Lark is called Daniel in one kitchen scene and Derek during the rain argument.
- geography drift: Sienna Sutton says Bellweather is in Vermont, then calls it outside Atlanta after two glasses of wine.
- mortality drift: A dead best friend appears at brunch and nobody agrees whether this is denial, evil, or scheduling.
- property drift: The basement belongs to a condo, a lake house, and an abandoned vineyard at different times in Bellweather.

Seed dialogue:
- "You think because you wear flannel and own a dock light I am supposed to trust you, Elias?"
- "Mothers do not disappear, Rachel. They evaporate into paperwork."
- "I checked his social media likes, Sienna. Nobody who enjoys motivational quotes is innocent."
- "The blood-adjacent tea towel is not decorative anymore and you know it."
- "Trust is just fear with a casserole dish, Sienna."
- "You seem upset," he said, making tea in a way that felt prosecutable.
- "If that basement is innocent, why does it sound defensive?"
- "I did not lie. I just told the truth in the order this town could survive."
